entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The most crucial contemporary environmental issue causing complicated alterations in terrestrial and aquatic microbial populations

entities.name: MICROBES
entities.description: Terrestrial and aquatic microbial populations that regulate greenhouse gas fluxes and respond to climate change

entities.name: CARBON DIOXIDE
entities.description: A biogenic greenhouse gas (CO2) whose rising levels contribute to climate change and are regulated by microbes

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GASES
entities.description: Atmospheric gases, including carbon dioxide, nitrous oxide, and methane, that affect soil microbial communities and drive climate change

entities.name: TEMPERATURE TRENDS
entities.description: Changing temperature patterns that contribute to climate change and affect soil microbial communities

entities.name: GLOBAL WARMING
entities.description: A phenomenon resulting from greenhouse gases that directly and indirectly affects soil microbial communities

entities.name: NITROUS OXIDE
entities.description: A significant biogenic greenhouse gas regulated by microbial interactions

entities.name: METHANE
entities.description: A significant biogenic greenhouse gas regulated by microbial interactions

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC VARIABLES
entities.description: Various factors that have intricate interactions with microbes

entities.name: MICROBIAL PROCESSES
entities.description: Biological activities of microbes that can be regulated to combat climate change by lowering greenhouse gas emissions

entities.name: MICROBIAL COMMUNITY STRUCTURE AND COMPOSITION
entities.description: The arrangement and makeup of microbial populations acting as a key feedback response mechanism for climate change
